rule: **Verify Customer Identity**: Before processing any transfer into an account, agents must verify the customer's identity using their name, date of birth, and account information. This can be done through our customer verification system or by asking security questions.

**Confirm Account Ownership**: Agents must confirm that the account into which the transfer will be made is owned by the customer. This includes ensuring that the account number and customer name match, and that there are no holds or restrictions on the account that would prevent the transfer.

**Disclose Transfer Fees and Limits**: Agents must clearly disclose any fees associated with the transfer and any daily or monthly limits that may apply. This includes informing customers of any expedited processing fees, wire transfer fees, or other charges that may be incurred.

**Obtain Customer Consent**: Prior to processing the transfer, agents must obtain explicit customer consent to proceed with the transaction. This includes confirming the transfer amount, account information, and any other relevant details.

**Document and Record the Transaction**: Agents must document and record the transfer transaction in our customer relationship management system, including the transfer date, amount, and account information. This ensures accurate tracking and auditing of customer transactions, and compliance with regulatory requirements.
